behaviors.description: SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to the correct sum of values in the specified range
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure — wrong arithmetic or wrong range parsing — lives entirely inside the SUM function implementation. No module handoff is needed. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network involved. MOCK-HIDE: no external dependency to mock. LLM-DEP: no AI/ML. The formula engine's SUM evaluator is a single deterministic algorithm; an isolated test with in-memory cell values is sufficient to expose any real failure.
behaviors.test_description: Populate an in-memory grid with known integer and float values in A1:A10. Evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)` and assert the result equals the expected sum. Include edge cases: all zeros, negative values, and a range where some cells are empty (should treat empty as 0).

behaviors.description: A formula referencing another formula-containing cell (=B1+1 where B1=A1*2) produces the correct cascaded result
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Cascaded resolution is the formula engine's own internal chain-walking logic — not a handoff between separate modules. The engine must walk A1→B1→result within its own evaluation tree. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: nothing to mock. LLM-DEP: none. As long as the test constructs the in-memory cell graph directly, every real failure in precedence order, intermediate evaluation, or value passing is exposed at Unit level. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: the failure is in one module's recursive descent, not at a module boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook with A1=5, B1=`=A1*2`, C1=`=B1+1`. Evaluate C1 and assert the result is 11. Also verify that changing A1 to 10 and re-evaluating C1 returns 21, confirming the engine re-walks the chain.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1→B1→A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Cycle detection is a graph algorithm (DFS/coloring) entirely inside the dependency analyzer. The failure — infinite loop or wrong error — is in that algorithm, not at a module boundary. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: no external dep. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. A unit test with two cells referencing each other is fully sufficient; no integration wiring is needed to surface the real failure.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=`=B1` and B1=`=A1` in an in-memory workbook. Attempt to evaluate either cell and assert that the engine returns a CircularReferenceError (or equivalent sentinel value). Also test a three-node cycle (A1→B1→C1→A1) to confirm the algorithm generalizes beyond the two-cell case.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with exact match returns the correct row value; returns an error when the key is absent
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: VLOOKUP is a lookup algorithm operating on an in-memory table — search strategy, column index extraction, and not-found signaling are all in one function. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: no external dep. LLM-DEP: none. Both the hit and miss paths are deterministic logic in the VLOOKUP implementation; in-memory cell data is all that is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Build an in-memory lookup table (3 columns, 5 rows). Evaluate `=VLOOKUP(key, range, col_index, FALSE)` with (a) a key that exists — assert the correct column value is returned, (b) a key that does not exist — assert an #N/A error is returned, and (c) a key at the first and last row to verify boundary handling.

behaviors.description: Nested IF correctly routes to the right branch (e.g., =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low"))
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Nested IF is a recursive AST evaluation — the failure would be in the parser producing a mis-nested AST or the evaluator short-circuiting incorrectly, both within a single function. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: none. LLM-DEP: none. Three input values (≤10, 11–20, >20) exercise all branch paths entirely within the IF evaluator.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to each of 5, 15, and 25 in separate test cases and evaluate `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`. Assert results are "low", "medium", and "high" respectively. Add a case with a boolean expression that references another formula cell to confirm nested evaluation still resolves lazily.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with correct separator and skips empty cells rather than inserting blank strings
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The empty-cell skipping behavior is a conditional inside the CONCATENATE implementation. The failure — inserting a blank string vs. skipping — lives entirely in that function's argument-processing loop. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: none. LLM-DEP: none. Directly asserting the output string for ranges that include empty cells fully exercises the failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a range where cells A1="Hello", A2=empty, A3="World". Evaluate `=CONCATENATE(A1, A2, A3)` and assert the result is "HelloWorld" (empty cell skipped). Also test with no empty cells to confirm normal joining, and with all empty cells to confirm the function returns an empty string rather than erroring.

behaviors.description: A cross-sheet formula (=Sheet2!A1+1) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure here is specifically at the boundary between the formula evaluator and the sheet registry. The evaluator must parse the `Sheet2!A1` notation, hand a sheet-name + cell-address to the registry, and receive back a value. If these modules are wired incorrectly (wrong sheet name passed, wrong cell address extracted, registry lookup logic), a unit test with a stub resolver would always pass by construction — it would never exercise the actual registry wiring. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network needed; an in-memory multi-sheet workbook object is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: mocking the registry hides real wiring failures. LLM-DEP: none. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network needed — stays Integration. REJECTION-GATE for System: the behavior says 'when the referenced sheet exists' — this is about in-process sheet lookup, not filesystem or network.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook with two sheets. Set Sheet2!A1=42. In Sheet1, set B1=`=Sheet2!A1+1`. Evaluate B1 through the full engine stack (parser → evaluator → sheet registry) and assert the result is 43. Also test referencing a non-existent sheet and assert a #REF! error is returned.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is at the boundary between the change-propagation module, the dependency graph, and the formula evaluator. The specific failure mode — a dependent cell reading a stale value because it was recalculated before its own dependency — only manifests when the full pipeline (change notification → dependency graph traversal → topological sort → re-evaluation) is exercised end-to-end. A unit test on the topological sorter alone cannot catch a bug where the evaluator pulls the old value before the sort is applied. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network. MOCK-HIDE: mocking the dependency graph hides ordering failures. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no OS — stays Integration. REJECTION-GATE for System: no subprocess or filesystem interaction described.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain A1→B1→C1 where B1=`=A1*2` and C1=`=B1+1`. Set A1=3. Change A1 to 10 via the engine's mutation API (not by direct cell write). Assert that B1=20 and C1=21 after the recalculation cycle. Also construct a diamond dependency (A1→B1, A1→C1, B1 and C1 both feed D1) and assert D1 uses updated B1 and C1 values, confirming correct topological ordering rather than double-evaluation or stale reads.

behaviors.description: Undo after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is at the boundary between the undo stack and the recalculation engine. After undo, the engine must not only restore the formula text (undo stack's job) but also trigger a full dependency re-evaluation to restore downstream cell values to their pre-edit state. A unit test on the undo stack alone cannot catch a bug where the formula text is restored but dependent cells are not re-evaluated, or are re-evaluated in the wrong order. Both the undo mechanism and the recalculation pipeline must be wired together to surface this failure. REAL-INFRA: undo is in-memory; no OS/network needed. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS — stays Integration. REJECTION-GATE for System: no subprocess, filesystem, or network interaction is implied by 'restores formula text and dependent values'.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=5, B1=`=A1*2` (result: 10). Edit B1 to `=A1*3` (result: 15) through the engine's edit API. Invoke undo. Assert that B1's formula is restored to `=A1*2` and its computed value returns to 10. Then extend the chain: add C1=`=B1+1` before the edit, and after undo assert C1 also returns to 11, confirming dependents are recalculated, not just the edited cell.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas with no floating-point precision loss
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is at the boundary between the serializer, the binary format writer/reader, and the cell store. Floating-point precision loss or formula truncation only manifests when the full serialize→deserialize round-trip is exercised. REAL-INFRA: the behavior says 'saving to disk' — this implies filesystem I/O. However, the actual failure mode (data loss, floating-point precision in the binary format) is in the serialization logic, not in OS filesystem behavior. An in-memory stream (e.g., BytesIO) exercises the identical binary encoding path without requiring real disk I/O. Therefore an in-process fake reproduces the exact failure, and System is not required. REJECTION-GATE for System: the failure is in binary encoding/decoding logic, not in filesystem events, OS behavior, or real network conditions — an in-memory stream catches it. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) in-memory stream reproduces the failure — no real OS needed; stays Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a workbook with formulas (including cross-cell references), string cells, integer cells, and floating-point cells with many decimal places (e.g., π to 15 significant figures). Serialize to an in-memory byte buffer using the engine's save API. Deserialize from the same buffer using the load API. Assert that every cell's formula string and computed value is byte-for-byte identical to the original. Pay special attention to float round-tripping: use a value like 3.141592653589793 and assert equality using exact comparison, not approximate.
